steps.title: Look for a contradiction
steps.content:
An equation has no solution when simplifying cancels the variable and leaves a false statement. The equation with the same variable term on both sides but different constants is:
$$
-13x-\frac{1}{9}=-13x+\frac{1}{9}
$$

steps.title: Cancel the variable terms
steps.content: Add $13x$ to both sides of that equation: $$ -\frac{1}{9}=\frac{1}{9} $$ This is false, so that equation has no solution. The answer is B.
